codex:
## DIRECTIVE

Retain the field-level novelty floor and the requested transition-\(L^1\)-Hölder kernel. Replace the current promissory C1–C3 objects as follows.

**C1 — concrete direct-backward sequential DR/HOIF estimator.** Remove \(\widehat g,\widehat T\), the current \(\mathcal R_{12}\), and any instruction to regress \(\widehat m_2(H_2)\) alone. Use nested cross-fitting. On a nuisance fold fit \(\widehat\pi_1,\widehat\pi_2,\widehat m_2\); on an independent backward-regression fold form
\[
\widehat D_2
=\widehat m_2(H_2)+{A_2\over\widehat\pi_2(H_2)}
 \{Y-\widehat m_2(H_2)\},
\]
and regress \(\widehat D_2\) on \(H_1\) among \(A_1=1\), using a boundary-corrected wavelet sieve, to obtain \(\widehat m_1\). On the evaluation fold define
\[
\widehat\psi^{\rm SDR}
=\mathbb P_n\!\left[
 \widehat m_1(H_1)+{A_1\over\widehat\pi_1(H_1)}
 \{\widehat D_2-\widehat m_1(H_1)\}\right].
\]
Define, conditional on the nuisance fold,
\[
b_1^\dagger(h_1)
=E_P[\widehat D_2\mid H_1=h_1,A_1=1].
\]
State the exact bias identity
\[
E_P[\widehat\psi^{\rm SDR}-\psi\mid{\cal T}]
=
E_{Q_1}\!\left[
 \left(1-{\pi_1\over\widehat\pi_1}\right)
 (\widehat m_1-b_1^\dagger)\right]
+
E_{Q_2}\!\left[
 \left(1-{\pi_2\over\widehat\pi_2}\right)
 (\widehat m_2-m_2)\right],
\]
where \(Q_1=P_{H_1}\) and
\[
Q_2(dh_2)=\int P_{H_1}(dh_1)g(h_2\mid h_1)\,dh_2.
\]
This is the required elimination of the pseudo-outcome mixed remainder: there is no term \((\pi_1-\widehat\pi_1)\widehat T(\widehat m_2-m_2)\).

Define the HOIF addition as the sum of two standard projected bilinear-remainder corrections, plus the sequential transport correction needed to implement \(Q_2\) from observed trajectories:

1. project the first displayed bilinear product in \(L^2(Q_1)\);
2. project the second in \(L^2(Q_2)\), representing \(Q_2\)-integration by cross-fitted \(A_1/\widehat\pi_1(H_1)\) weights;
3. correct the resulting transport-weight residual by the chained period-1 score kernel.

Use boundary-corrected wavelet vectors \(Z_{j,K_j}\), independently estimated empirical Gram matrices
\[
\widehat\Omega_1=\mathbb P_{\cal G}[Z_{1,K_1}Z_{1,K_1}^{T}],\qquad
\widehat\Omega_2=\mathbb P_{\cal G}\!\left[
 {A_1\over\widehat\pi_1(H_1)}Z_{2,K_2}Z_{2,K_2}^{T}\right],
\]
and kernels \(K_j(x,x')=Z_{j,K_j}(x)^T\widehat\Omega_j^{-1}Z_{j,K_j}(x')\). Write every order-\(q\) correction as an explicit symmetrized degenerate U-statistic chain of observable propensity and outcome/pseudo-outcome residuals joined by these kernels. State its sign, normalization \((n)_q^{-1}\), and fold provenance. Do not leave “derive a correction” as a definition.

Set
\[
K_j\asymp n^{\,2d/\{2s_j+d\}},\qquad
s_1=\alpha_1+t,\quad s_2=\alpha_2+\beta,
\]
with block-truncation if required for \(K_j>n\). Define \(q_j\) as the least integer satisfying the displayed preliminary-nuisance remainder inequality needed to make the uncorrected Neumann tail \(o(n^{-r_j})\); the proposal must state that inequality and the nuisance rates used in it. The higher-order target is the two projected stage-local bilinear remainders and the observable \(Q_2\)-transport residual—not estimation of \(g\), \(T\), or \(\widehat T(\widehat m_2-m_2)\).

**C2 — two explicit calibrated fuzzy experiments.** Replace the present single mixture by two separately calibrated finite experiments, one for each exponent. For both, state the entire longitudinal factorization
\[
p_1(h_1)p(a_1\mid h_1)p(h_2\mid h_1,a_1)
p(a_2\mid h_2,a_1)p(y\mid h_2,a_1,a_2),
\]
all untreated/off-regime factors, the binary-\(Y\) probabilities, and an explicit full-data extension constructed from independent exogenous uniforms so consistency and both exchangeability statements hold.

For the \(\alpha_1+t\) experiment fix \(p_1\equiv1\), \(\pi_2\equiv1/2\), a bounded analytic mean-zero \(v(h_2)\), and a fixed bounded \(m_2=\delta v\). With disjoint incoming bumps \(u_{j,h}\), define
\[
\pi_{1,z}(h_1)=\frac12+\eta\sum_jz_ju_{j,h}(h_1),
\]
\[
g_{z,\lambda}(h_2\mid h_1)
=1+\epsilon\sum_j
 {\lambda_j u_{j,h}(h_1)\over1+2\eta z_ju_{j,h}(h_1)}\,v(h_2).
\]
Disjoint support makes this unambiguous, \(\int g\,dh_2=1\), and
\[
\pi_{1,z}g_{z,\lambda}
=\pi_{1,z}+{\epsilon\over2}\sum_j\lambda_ju_{j,h}v
\]
on each bump. Mix uniformly over \(z\), with \(\lambda_j=+z_j\) under \(\nu_+\) and \(\lambda_j=-z_j\) under \(\nu_-\). State the exact target separation
\[
\left|E_{\nu_+}\psi-E_{\nu_-}\psi\right|
=
4\eta\epsilon\delta\!\left(\int v^2\right)
\sum_j\int {u_{j,h}^2\over1-4\eta^2u_{j,h}^2}
\asymp\eta\epsilon.
\]
Calibrate
\[
J\asymp h^{-d},\quad
\eta=c_\eta h^{\alpha_1},\quad
\epsilon=c_\epsilon h^t,\quad
h\asymp n^{-2/\{2(\alpha_1+t)+d\}},
\]
and state the affinity calculation to be proved as
\[
n^2\eta^2\epsilon^2/J=O(1).
\]
This yields separation \(n^{-2(\alpha_1+t)/\{2(\alpha_1+t)+d\}}\).

For the \(\alpha_2+\beta\) experiment fix the first-period law and transition. At \(H_2\), use the analogous exactly paired construction
\[
\pi_{2,z}=\frac12+a\sum_jz_jv_{j,h},\qquad
m_{2,z,\lambda}
=b\sum_j{\lambda_jv_{j,h}\over1+2az_jv_{j,h}},
\]
with \(Y\in\{-1,1\}\) having conditional mean \(m_{2,z,\lambda}\), and mix over \(\lambda=\pm z\). Set
\[
J\asymp h^{-d},\quad a\asymp h^{\alpha_2},\quad
b\asymp h^\beta,\quad
h\asymp n^{-2/\{2(\alpha_2+\beta)+d\}},
\]
state \(n^2a^2b^2/J=O(1)\), and compute the exact separation \(\asymp ab=n^{-r_2}\).

For each experiment list positivity bounds, Hölder norm bounds, transition normalization, \(Y\)-probability validity, component-law membership, the mixing measures, product mixtures \(\int P_\theta^{\otimes n}d\nu_\pm\), and the exact Hellinger/chi-square quantity to be bounded. Delete the baseline \(H_1\)-density perturbation. It is invalid for the claimed first exponent unless its amplitude is explicitly included in the target and affinity calibration; doing so produces a three-smoothness signal and does not establish the stated \(\alpha_1+t\) rate.

**C3 — explicit finite-\(t\) structural-separation node.** Add a separately numbered proposition/conjecture defining
\[
{\cal G}^{t_0}_{\rm AH}(M,\theta_0)
=
\left\{1+\theta w(h_1)v_0(h_2):
 \|w\|_{C^{t_0}}\le M,\ |\theta|\le\theta_0\right\},
\]
where \(t_0>d/2\) is fixed and finite, \(v_0(h_2)=\sqrt2\cos(2\pi h_{2,1})\) is analytic, bounded, and mean-zero, and \(\theta_0\) ensures positive densities. Make the class genuinely nontrivial by allowing an infinite-dimensional \(C^{t_0}\) ball for \(w\) and exhibit an exact-\(t_0\) member, for noninteger \(t_0\), using a smooth cutoff times \(|h_{1,1}-1/2|^{t_0}\), which is not in \(C^{t_0+\xi}\) for any specified \(\xi>0\). Do not call a wholly analytic incoming-state class “finite smoothness”: analytic functions are smoother than every finite Hölder order. State explicitly that on this non-state-independent subclass, if \(\alpha_2+\beta\ge d/2\), then the proposed frontier predicts root-\(n\) estimation for every \(\alpha_1>0\), because \(\alpha_1+t_0>d/2\).

**Closest-comparator and consumer repair.** Add Bonvini–Kennedy–Keele (2025, EcoSta Abstract A0576) as the closest comparator and use an object-by-object table: model regularity, whether a transition density is estimated, recursive pseudo-outcome correction, stated rate channels, lower bound, and structural separation. Their public abstract establishes a two-time-point g-formula HOIF analysis under anisotropic and additive structure with minimax optimality in some settings; it does not expose enough assumptions for a lawful set-inclusion claim. Therefore claim only the exact additions stated here: incoming-state \(C^t(L^1(dh_2))\) transition regularity without outgoing-state density smoothness, direct-DR backward regression without fitting \(g\), the two-channel \((\alpha_1+t,\alpha_2+\beta)\) frontier, and the legally normalized chained converse. Do not claim strict non-inclusion in their class unless a full manuscript supplies the assumptions needed to prove it.

Replace every generic “consumer” for C1, C2, and C3 with: “the model-specific non-root-\(n\) optimality problem for time-varying treatment effects posed by Cinelli, Feller, Imbens, Kennedy, Magliacane, and Zubizarreta (2025, §3.5.3), instantiated for the named two-decision physiologic-challenge regime.” Do not lower the novelty floor.

## RATIONALE

The direct DR pseudo-outcome changes the first regression’s population target from \(Tm_2\) to \(b_1^\dagger\). That produces the displayed two-term bias identity and removes the recurring estimated-\(m_2\) cross-period remainder without requiring a transition-density estimator. A sequential transport correction is still needed because \(Q_2\) is interventional; it should be named rather than hidden inside \(\widehat T\).

The proposed rational transition pairing is normalized and makes the treated joint density cancel algebraically while retaining an \(\eta\epsilon\) target signal. In contrast, the present baseline-density perturbation contributes an additional smoothness/amplitude channel and cannot prove the advertised \(\alpha_1+t\) exponent.

The public Bonvini–Kennedy–Keele description already covers two-time-point g-formula HOIF estimation and reports minimax optimality in some anisotropic or structured settings, so generic “first longitudinal HOIF frontier” language is unsafe. The defensible distinction is the exact transition-\(L^1\)-Hölder model, estimator architecture, two-channel rate, and chained converse specified above. [EcoSta Abstract A0576](https://www.cmstatistics.org/RegistrationsV2/EcoSta2025/viewSubmission.php?in=576&token=8561n129qq6qo72qo5o1nq664nn5pqpo)

Cinelli et al. provide the correct named external consumer: optimality outside the root-\(n\) regime for time-varying treatments. [Cinelli et al., “A Dozen Challenges in Causality and Causal Inference”](https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.17099)
why: The fourth review remained at field tier but exposed a recurring well-posedness defect and newly relevant closest prior art. This exact directive is necessary before the final current-angle revision/pivot; preserve the field novelty floor and do not accept a proposal omitting C3 or a legal alpha1+t converse.

tried: Persisted a corrected mathematical directive after angle1-v1: retain the exact b1-dagger-centered identity but withdraw the claim that it eliminates the mixed transport error; replace the faulty stage-two and scalar transport kernels with a first-period-weighted stage-two residual and a two-index bridge chain; relabel the matched rate as a conjecture; preserve C2/C3 as proof obligations; and handle state sufficiency as an unvalidated structural restriction.
codex: The exact conditional bias has two equivalent forms: E[hat psi^SDR-psi|F_TB]=E_Q1[(1-w1)(hat m1-b1^dagger)]+E_Q2[(1-w2)e2] and E[hat psi^SDR-psi|F_TB]=E_Q1[(1-w1)(hat m1-m1)]+E_Q1[w1 T_g{(1-w2)e2}]. Thus the reviewer’s weighted expression is present in expansion (B), but it does not make (A) algebraically false once b1^dagger and the conditioning folds are stated correctly. What is false is the interpretation that direct backward regression eliminates the cross-period mixed error. The minimal transport term is therefore a three-error product: first-period propensity error times the transported second-period propensity–outcome product. Direct pseudo-outcome regression transforms/absorbs B_T into hat m1-b1^dagger; it does not eliminate it. Removing it exactly without estimating g requires an observable higher-order chain joining the two periods. The stage-two and transport chains are not correct as written. A bare internal R2 changes the integration measure, the current transport chain omits the stage-two Neumann direction, and its shortest-chain sign is wrong. These defects invalidate the present attaining-estimator theorem, but not the underlying two-channel conjecture.
why: The reviewer correctly challenged the interpretation and current kernels but incorrectly treated the b1-dagger-centered two-term identity as algebraically false. A fresh mandatory gpt-5.6-sol medium audit derived both equivalent expansions and located the actual defect in the correction architecture. The next revision must reflect that distinction and must not promote the rate to a theorem.

Define A_AH^{t_0}=G_AH^{t_0}(M,theta_0) intersect G_d^{t_0}(M) and M_AH={P in M(alpha_1,alpha_2,beta,t_0;M,c):g in A_AH^{t_0}}. codex:
IMPROVE: No treatment-count-adaptive block, ridge, generalized inverse, or deterministic/reference-Gram substitution currently closes the matched frontier under the same assumptions. Adaptive blocks remove the algebraic rank defect but still require a uniform projection/transport approximation theorem; ridge or pseudoinversion leaves uncontrolled null-space bias when \(K_2>N_1\); and a reference Gram would require control of the unknown, merely bounded \(Q_2\) design or an additional density assumption. Do not promote any of these into an upper theorem.

D0 directive:

1. Add an explicit same-assumption first-order upper-bracket theorem. Fully specify fixed-proportion cross-fitting; boundary-corrected wavelet least-squares fits with dimensions
\[
k_{\pi_j}\asymp n^{d/(2\alpha_j+d)},\quad
k_{m_2}\asymp n^{d/(2\beta+d)},\quad
k_{m_1}\asymp n^{d/(2t+d)};
\]
clip propensities to \([c/2,1-c/2]\), \(m_2,m_1\) to fixed bounded ranges, and use the displayed augmented pseudo-outcome. Prove uniformly over the nonempty stated model that
\[
\|\widehat\pi_j-\pi_j\|_2\lesssim n^{-\gamma_j},\quad
\|\widehat m_2-m_2\|_2\lesssim n^{-\gamma_b},\quad
\|\widehat m_1-b_1^\dagger\|_2\lesssim n^{-\gamma_t},
\]
where \(\gamma_j=\alpha_j/(2\alpha_j+d)\), \(\gamma_b=\beta/(2\beta+d)\), and \(\gamma_t=t/(2t+d)\). Use overlap and bounded design densities to justify equivalence of all treated-regression designs, and use \(g\in C^t(L^1)\) plus bounded pseudo-outcomes to show \(b_1^\dagger\) has a uniform \(C^t\) radius. Combining the exact conditional bias identity with Cauchy–Schwarz and the bounded evaluation-fold variance must yield
\[
\mathfrak R_n(\mathcal M)
\lesssim n^{-1/2}
+n^{-(\gamma_1+\gamma_t)}
+n^{-(\gamma_2+\gamma_b)}.
\]
State the honest two-sided bracket
\[
\max\{n^{-1/2},n^{-r_1},n^{-r_2}\}
\lesssim\mathfrak R_n(\mathcal M)
\lesssim n^{-1/2}
+n^{-(\gamma_1+\gamma_t)}
+n^{-(\gamma_2+\gamma_b)}.
\]
This is not a matched frontier. It gives only the sufficient root-\(n\) conditions \(\gamma_1+\gamma_t\ge1/2\) and \(\gamma_2+\gamma_b\ge1/2\), not the conjectured \(s_j\ge d/2\) conditions.

2. Restore `lem:gformula` and `lem:direct-bias` as proved standalone results in the rendered theorem graph. They need not be dependencies of the converse. The direct-bias identity is essential to the new upper bracket and is already advertised in `tldr`, `fill`, and `honest_scope`; pruning it creates a false paper/prose mismatch. Include both equivalent bias forms and explicitly say direct backward regression relocates rather than eliminates the transported mixed remainder.

3. Strengthen the rank result to the deterministic statement
\[
\operatorname{rank}(\widehat\Omega_{2,b})\le N_1
\]
for every block and every choice of nonzero weights, hence every block of dimension \(k_b>N_1\) is singular. Add the general corollary that for \(\pi_1\equiv p\) and \(k_b/m\to\lambda>p\), singularity occurs with probability tending to one. Explain that ridge and pseudoinversion cannot restore the missing block directions, while treatment-count-adaptive blocks address rank only and do not prove the required approximation, operator-norm, U-statistic variance, or two-index transport-tail bounds.

4. Rename the “scalar-correction-order frontier” throughout as a “scalar correction-order envelope” or “staircase theorem.” Preserve its exact minimal-set and rectangle results, but never suggest they control the stochastic HOIF chains.

5. Audit every headline and interpretation field. The title, abstract-style prose, theorem justifications, consumers, and honest-scope section must call the result a transition-mediated converse plus an explicit first-order bracket and frozen-estimator refutation. They must not say “sharp,” “matched,” “determined,” “frontier theorem,” or imply that C1/C3 establishes root-\(n\) estimability. Keep the finite-\(t\) geometry’s rate implication expressly conditional.

6. Reframe novelty at the field floor. Robins et al. already cover HOIFs for time-dependent treatment, and the Bonvini–Kennedy–Keele abstract already announces two-time-point g-formula higher-order estimators and minimax optimality in some anisotropic/structured models. Claim novelty only for the incoming-state \(C^t(L^1)\) transition-mediated lower channel, the normalized chained fuzzy converse, the exact sequential remainder identity and first-order bracket, the scalar staircase, and the concrete empirical-Gram failure. This is potentially publishable as a focused field-level theory note after these changes, but not as a solved minimax-frontier paper.

Synchronize `tldr`, `project_justification.gap`, `project_justification.niche`, `project_justification.fill`, and `related_work` whenever the headline changes. codex:
IMPROVE: The bracket and converse are substantively valid, but two concrete same-assumption repairs are required before D0.5.

1. Restore `lem:gformula` as a proved dependency. `def:gformula-objects` defines the observed-data functional but does not prove that it equals \(E_{\widetilde P}[Y(1,1)]\). Prove the equality by iterated expectation using first- and second-period exchangeability, overlap, consistency, and outcome-state sufficiency. Make `lem:direct-bias` depend on `lem:gformula`; this keeps the identification lemma reachable without artificially attaching it to the converse theorem. Synchronize this dependency in `proto_core.json`, `core.json`, `d0_working.json`, and `writeup.tex`.

2. Repair the fourth-moment wavelet proof. The present assertion that boundedness of \(W\) and \(f\) controls moments of the score around the selected-design projection \(f_k\) is incomplete: bounded design-density ratios alone do not establish a uniform bound for that weighted projection. Instead use a deterministic boundary-wavelet approximant \(f_k^0\) satisfying
\[
\|f-f_k^0\|_{L^2(Q)}\lesssim k^{-s/d},\qquad
\|f-f_k^0\|_\infty\lesssim k^{-s/d},\qquad
\|f_k^0\|_\infty\lesssim1.
\]
On the empirical-Gram good event, expand the normal equations around \(f_k^0\); separate the conditionally centered bounded noise \(W-f(X)\) from the uniformly bounded approximation residual \(f-f_k^0\). Apply the fixed-moment vector-sum bound to obtain \(\sqrt{k/m}\), control the deterministic approximation term by \(k^{-s/d}\), and use clipping plus the exponential Gram tail on the complement. State the conditional version explicitly so it applies uniformly to the random \(b_{1,v}^\dagger\) given \(\mathcal F_{T_v}\). This validates the fourth moments used in the conditional-bias-to-RMSE step.

Also replace C3’s phrases “claimed two-channel upper frontier” and “proposed root-\(n\) elbow inequalities” by “conjectured matched two-channel rate” and “necessary lower-channel elbow inequalities”; retain the explicit conditional-future-theorem qualification. The rank lemma, scalar staircase, fixed-proportion sample map, Moore–Penrose definition, treated-design equivalences, \(b_1^\dagger\) regularity, qualified lower bracket, and uniform upper claim otherwise check out. No matched-rate or HOIF improvement is responsibly available under the same assumptions. No headline change is required, so the synchronized TL;DR, gap/niche/fill, related work, interpretation, and honest scope should remain unchanged. After these repairs, the focused result plausibly remains at the field novelty floor.

codex:
BOUNDED_REPAIR: The novelty finding is curable; the negative-target finding is correct. Cinelli et al. §3.5.3 identifies non-root-\(n\) optimality for time-varying treatments as open “regardless of model setup”; this theorem makes a truthful field-level partial advance by proving a model-specific necessary exponent and bracket, while not solving optimality. [Cinelli et al.](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2508.17099)

Exact D0 directive:

- Split `thm:transition-mediated-converse-first-order-bracket`: retain the qualified \(n^{-r_1}\) incoming-transition converse, standard \(n^{-r_2}\) reduction, parametric floor, explicit first-order upper bound, and the gap between necessary and sufficient elbows. Remove the empirical-Gram obstruction from this headline theorem.
- Add a theorem-level scope/comparison corollary stating:
  1. Cinelli §3.5.3 asks for non-root-\(n\) optimality for time-varying treatments;
  2. the theorem partially answers that agenda for the specified incoming-state \(C^t(L^1)\) model by establishing the new necessary channel \(r_1=2(\alpha_1+t)/(2(\alpha_1+t)+d)\) and an explicit nonmatching bracket;
  3. it does not determine the minimax rate or root-\(n\) iff boundary.
- Compare object-by-object: Robins et al. provide HOIF machinery applicable to time-dependent treatment but do not state this transition class or \(r_1\) theorem; the Bonvini–Kennedy–Keele abstract announces two-time-point anisotropic/additive HOIF results and minimaxity in some settings, but exposes insufficient assumptions for class inclusion, theorem equivalence, non-overlap, or priority claims. [Robins et al.](https://arxiv.org/abs/1601.05820), [Bonvini–Kennedy–Keele abstract](https://www.cmstatistics.org/RegistrationsV2/EcoSta2025/viewSubmission.php?in=576&token=8561n129qq6qo72qo5o1nq664nn5pqpo)

Do not claim “first,” priority, a sharp frontier, minimax optimality, comparator non-inclusion, or novelty for the standard terminal channel.

codex:
HARD_ESCALATION: The mathematical defect is locally repairable, but the field-novelty floor is not truthfully establishable from the available public comparator record.

For `lem:geometric-transport-index-set`, replace the possibly empty interval argument by choosing any omitted index \((p_0,r_0)\) and taking the minimum positive excess over the finite sublevel set
\[
\left\{(p,r):
r_\star<
\gamma_b+(p+1)\gamma_1+(r+1)\gamma_2
\le
\gamma_b+(p_0+1)\gamma_1+(r_0+1)\gamma_2
\right\}.
\]
This set contains \((p_0,r_0)\), so it is nonempty; positivity of \(\gamma_1,\gamma_2\) makes it finite. Its minimum excess \(\delta>0\) justifies the geometric-tail bound. This is a finite, same-assumption proof repair.

The novelty defect is different:

- The closest public Bonvini–Kennedy–Keele source says that it studies the two-time-point g-formula under anisotropic and additive smoothness models, constructs higher-order estimators, calculates bias and variance, and proves minimax optimality in some settings. It supplies no definitions of its nuisance classes, transition restrictions, theorem statements, or rates. Therefore it cannot support class inclusion, equivalence, exclusion, non-overlap, or a rate comparison. [EcoSta primary abstract](https://www.cmstatistics.org/RegistrationsV2/EcoSta2025/viewSubmission.php?in=576&token=8561n129qq6qo72qo5o1nq664nn5pqpo)
- The authors’ public research listing does not provide a manuscript containing the missing assumptions or theorems. [Bonvini research page](https://www.matteobonvini.com/research.html)
- Robins et al. explicitly cover HOIF methods for time-dependent treatment with time-varying confounding, but their public record does not establish that the present incoming-state \(C^t(L^1)\) channel is a distinct field advance relative to the closer two-time-point work. [Robins et al. technical report](https://arxiv.org/abs/1601.05820)
- Cinelli et al. supplies the broad open agenda, but being a partial answer to an open agenda does not itself establish novelty over the undisclosed closer result. [Cinelli et al.](https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.17099)

Consequently, no truthful same-kernel, same-assumption finite repair can satisfy the panel. Doing so would require at least one forbidden or unavailable step: inventing Bonvini assumptions, inferring non-overlap from an abstract, claiming priority from nondisclosure, strengthening the model solely to manufacture separation, or closing the unmatched minimax frontier.

This is the second independent novelty-floor objection after the exact bounded recentering. Further D0 wording, demotion, or comparison-table loops cannot create the missing comparator information and would be nonproductive. Progress requires a newly available full comparator manuscript or a genuine kernel-level pivot/new theorem—not another same-kernel D0 repair.
why: Stop before F. The scalar proof gap is finite and repairable, but the field-novelty target cannot be certified from the available primary comparator record. Further same-kernel auto loops would either repeat the finding or launder unavailable information.

codex: VERDICT: FIELD-NO-GO. The incoming n^{-r_1} floor is a legal embedding of the 2009 one-time MAR hard family; variation dependence requires realizing its response regression through the transition but does not prevent transfer. D0.5 must not pass and F must not start.
why: Map the 2009 family by H_1=Z with density f_\lambda=a_\lambda/2, \pi_{1,\lambda}=1/a_\lambda, and g_\lambda(h_2\mid h_1)=(1-b_\lambda(h_1))k_0(h_2)+b_\lambda(h_1)k_1(h_2) for fixed half-cube densities. A fixed analytic m_2 with unequal k_0/k_1 means gives m_1=\mu_0+(\mu_1-\mu_0)b_\lambda and target separation equal to the Theorem 3.1 separation up to a nonzero constant. The embedded experiment satisfies incoming C^t(L^1), propensity C^{\alpha_1}, overlap, design-density, state-sufficiency, and causal-extension requirements and is Blackwell-equivalent to the MAR observation plus ancillary coordinates. Thus the only non-root regime yields exactly r_1=2(\alpha_1+t)/\{2(\alpha_1+t)+d\}; the terminal and parametric floors are standard, the upper/lower rates remain unmatched, and no sharp elbow is proved. The 2016 Theorem-49 rough-density noncoverage does not establish nontransfer from the 2009 lower theorem. BKK remains public-abstract-only with overlap unresolved.
